original_hypothesis.title: Retroactive Reward Rate Modification via setRewards for Unprocessed Past Epochs
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setRewards can modify `cantoPerBlock` for any epoch, including past epochs that have not yet been consumed by update_market. Since update_market is lazy (only processes blocks when explicitly called), governance can change reward rates for fully-elapsed epochs before any market processes them. When update_market finally runs for a stale market, it uses the modified (not original) rate, retroactively changing rewards that users expected to earn based on the original configuration.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This violates the principle that past rewards should be immutable once earned. Markets that haven't been updated recently are vulnerable to having their reward calculations altered retroactively, even by well-intentioned governance corrections.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setRewards writes to `cantoPerBlock[i]` for any epoch in range with no check that `i + BLOCK_EPOCH > block.number` (i.e., epoch hasn't fully elapsed); update_market reads cantoPerBlock lazily

preconditions: Governance calls setRewards with epoch range that includes past epochs
At least one market has lastRewardBlock before the modified epochs
No one has called update_market for those markets since the modified epochs

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Governance can silently rewrite past reward rates before markets process them
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires governance to call setRewards for past epochs
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Between epoch elapsing and update_market processing those blocks

operational_questions: How frequently is update_market called for active markets?
Has governance ever called setRewards for already-elapsed epochs?
What is the typical staleness of market lastRewardBlock values?
validation_requirements: Test setRewards modifying a past epoch followed by update_market consuming it
Confirm no guard prevents past-epoch modification
Measure impact on user rewards from retroactive rate changes

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Governance identifies markets with stale lastRewardBlock
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Governance calls setRewards to modify rates for unprocessed past epochs
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: When update_market is called, modified rates are applied retroactively
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Users receive different rewards than originally configured

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Governance can silently alter reward history
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Cannot undo retroactive changes after update_market processes them
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Reward distribution for all stale markets

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: Understanding of lazy evaluation and epoch mapping

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: setRewards calls targeting past epoch ranges
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Reward rate changes for elapsed epochs
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Governance modifying rewards before market updates

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
